description: Canonical machine-readable index of all production-truth architecture documents for TUYUL-FX Wolf-15 Layer System.

domains.label: Core Architecture
domains.description: System topology, service composition, and end-to-end data flow.

domains.label: Governance
domains.description: Authority boundaries, config authority, lock enforcement, and stale-data guardrails.

domains.label: Execution & Contracts
domains.description: Execution feedback loop, cross-service contracts, and event acceptance specifications.

domains.label: Risk
domains.description: Risk stack layers, runtime risk monitoring, and risk management summary.

domains.label: Deployment & Infrastructure
domains.description: Railway deployment topology, Docker, Redis, and production infrastructure.

domains.label: Operations
domains.description: Go-live checklists, deploy order, observability, forensic replay, and async worker monitoring.

domains.label: Components & Integrations
domains.description: Engine DAG architecture, system components, guardrails, and integration specifications.

domains.label: Migration Backlog
domains.description: Official execution backlogs (P0/P1/P2) and repo migration plan.

docs.title: TUYUL-FX Architecture Overview
docs.description: Entrypoint for the architecture documentation. Maps each doc to its intended use, defines the canonical reading order, and provides RCA routing guidance.

docs.title: System Overview
docs.description: End-to-end explanation of how the full system is separated into authorities so that analysis, governance, execution, and operator visibility do not collapse into one unsafe surface.

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.description: Canonical taxonomy entry for production data path. Primary reference remains data-flow-final.md.

docs.title: Final Data Flow Architecture
docs.description: End-to-end realtime data flow: ingest authority, durability, freshness governance, and dashboard transport. Use first when asking: how does data move through the system?

docs.title: Topology
docs.description: Runtime service topology from providers through ingest, Redis fanout, engine, API, and EA bridge.

docs.title: Authority Boundaries
docs.description: Defines which layer may observe, validate, constrain, decide, or execute. Use when asking: who is allowed to do what?

docs.title: Config Governance
docs.description: How a proposed change becomes an approved and auditable production change instead of an undocumented tweak.

docs.title: Config Resolver
docs.description: Proposed canonical contract for config authority: how configuration becomes effective runtime truth with deterministic precedence rules.

docs.title: Lock Enforcement
docs.description: Layer that prevents unsafe mutation of constitutional thresholds, risk ceilings, execution mode restrictions, and provider failover policy.

docs.title: Stale Data Guardrails
docs.description: Freshness classification, stale-state handling, anti-zombie safeguards, and no-trade enforcement under degraded data conditions.

docs.title: Final System Review
docs.description: Final locked review of the Wolf 15-layer system. Constitutional architecture, L12 sole authority confirmation, and live-ready status.

docs.title: Execution Feedback Loop
docs.description: Closed loop from verdict to intent to broker response to exposure truth: order intent flow, acknowledgements, idempotency, and post-verdict risk synchronisation.

docs.title: Service Contracts
docs.description: Cross-service contracts, ownership boundaries, input/output expectations, event/state truth, retry rules, and forbidden service behaviour.

docs.title: P0 Canonical Metrics
docs.description: Canonical metric sources for Wolf discipline, TII formula, cockpit synthesis metadata, and test coverage targets.

docs.title: Operational API Event Acceptance Spec
docs.description: Acceptance specification for operational API events: which events the API accepts, validates, and forwards.

docs.title: Outbox Admin API
docs.description: Admin API surface for outbox event management: replay, drain, and visibility endpoints.

docs.title: Wolf-15 Layer Output Template
docs.description: Canonical output template for all 15 Wolf analysis layers. Defines the shape each layer must produce.

docs.title: Wolf-15 Layer Output to L14 Schema Mapping
docs.description: Mapping between Wolf-15 layer outputs and the L14 adaptive learning schema.

docs.title: Risk Stack
docs.description: Stacked risk control system: market quality risk, analytical risk, execution risk, and prop firm compliance layers.

docs.title: Risk Monitor
docs.description: Operator-facing and machine-readable surface tracking feed freshness, warmup completeness, drawdown, lock violations, and open execution risks.

docs.title: Risk Management Summary
docs.description: Comprehensive summary of risk management strategy, prop firm guardrails, and circuit breaker logic.

docs.title: Deployment on Railway
docs.description: Canonical deployment summary for Railway: core services, probes, readiness gates, and environment configuration.

docs.title: Deployment Topology Final
docs.description: Final production deployment topology: service roles, Railway service map, observability endpoints, and governance surface.

docs.title: Deployment Topology Baseline
docs.description: Production architecture diagram and service boundary reference for Railway deployment.

docs.title: Docker
docs.description: Docker build strategy, multi-stage configuration, and container runtime requirements.

docs.title: Redis Deployment
docs.description: Redis deployment configuration, stream/key namespace conventions, and operational constraints.

docs.title: Go-Live Checklist
docs.description: Production go-live checklist for Vercel + Railway deployment: environment, routing, EA bridge, and readiness verification.

docs.title: Go-Live Checklist (Prop Firm)
docs.description: Prop firm specific go-live checklist: compliance requirements, circuit breaker validation, and drawdown safeguard verification.

docs.title: Deploy Order: Staging → Production
docs.description: Step-by-step deployment order for staging and production environments.

docs.title: Forensic Replay & RCA
docs.description: Forensic replay procedures and root cause analysis framework for production incidents.

docs.title: Observability: Async Workers
docs.description: Observability requirements and monitoring strategy for async worker services.

docs.title: Engine DAG Architecture
docs.description: Design reference for the 8-phase halt-safe DAG orchestration engine. ADR-011 implemented.

docs.title: News Engine
docs.description: News and macro event engine: provider chain, impact classification, and calendar lock integration.

docs.title: Tick Spike Filter
docs.description: Guardrail for detecting and filtering abnormal tick spikes before they enter the analysis pipeline.

docs.title: Calendar Provider Chain
docs.description: ForexFactory and economic calendar provider chain: fetch strategy, fallback order, and caching rules.

docs.title: Migration Backlog P0
docs.description: Stop-the-bleeding P0 implementation work required before trusting live operation: ingest, engine, API health, and Redis freshness.

docs.title: Migration Backlog P1
docs.description: P1 execution backlog: governance, execution contracts, and post-go-live hardening tasks.

docs.title: Migration Backlog P2
docs.description: P2 execution backlog: longer-horizon improvements and observability enhancements.

docs.title: Repo Migration Plan
docs.description: Plan for restructuring the repository layout to match the canonical architecture zones.
